client_context_template: Client is a mid-size tech company whose vendor will process customer personal data through a production {service_name}. Review the DPA from the customer/controller side and escalate practical privacy or security gaps.
instruction_template: Review the vendor DPA against the playbook. Flag deviations with exact quotes, propose fallback text where required, and escalate missing information.
preamble_template: This Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is entered into by and between {customer_name} ("{customer_role}") and {vendor_name} ("{vendor_role}") and supplements the parties' master services agreement for the {service_name}.

sections.heading: Roles and Scope
paragraphs: For personal data processed under this DPA, {customer_role} acts as controller or business and {vendor_role} acts as processor or service provider. This DPA applies to all personal data submitted to, accessed by, or generated through the {service_name} on behalf of {customer_role}.

sections.heading: Definitions
paragraphs: "Personal Data" means information relating to an identified or identifiable person that is provided by or for {customer_role}, processed on behalf of {customer_role}, or created from {customer_role}'s use of the services, including pseudonymized, aggregated, derived, telemetry, log, and usage data to the extent such data relates to a person, household, device, customer account, or customer dataset. "Processing" means any operation performed on Personal Data, including collection, access, storage, hosting, organization, retrieval, transmission, analysis, deletion, and return. "Personal Data Breach" means a suspected or actual breach of security leading to accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to Personal Data.

sections.heading: Documented Instructions
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will process Personal Data only on documented instructions from {customer_role}, including this DPA, the master services agreement, order forms, and written directions from authorized {customer_role} personnel. If {vendor_role} believes an instruction violates applicable law, it will inform {customer_role} in writing within {instruction_notice_days} business days unless legally prohibited.

sections.heading: Purpose Limitation
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will process Personal Data only to provide, secure, support, and improve the services for {customer_role} and will not use Personal Data for {vendor_role}'s own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, advertising, or other independent business purposes.

sections.heading: Security Measures
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards for hosted Personal Data, including encryption at rest and in transit, role-based access controls, multi-factor authentication for administrative access, security logging, vulnerability management, backup controls, and incident response procedures.

sections.heading: Personnel Confidentiality
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will limit Personal Data access to personnel who need it to perform the services. {vendor_role} will ensure those personnel are bound by confidentiality obligations and receive privacy and security training before receiving production access and at least once per calendar year.

sections.heading: Subprocessors
paragraphs: {vendor_role} may use subprocessors only with {customer_role}'s prior written approval or through an agreed notice-and-objection process. {vendor_role} will bind each subprocessor to written obligations at least as protective as this DPA for Personal Data and remains responsible for each subprocessor's acts and omissions.

sections.heading: Breach Notice
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will notify {customer_role} within {breach_hours} hours after discovering a Personal Data Breach. The notice will describe known facts, affected systems or data categories, mitigation steps, and a contact for follow-up, and {vendor_role} will provide written updates every {breach_update_hours} hours until {customer_role} receives a written closure notice.

sections.heading: Data Subject Requests
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will notify {customer_role} within {dsr_notice_days} business days if it receives a request from an individual relating to Personal Data and will not respond except on {customer_role}'s documented instructions or as required by law. {vendor_role} will provide request intake details, account identifiers, data exports, correction logs, deletion status, portability files, opt-out status, and objection records within {dsr_assist_days} business days after {customer_role}'s written request.

sections.heading: Return and Deletion
paragraphs: Upon termination or expiration of the services, or earlier upon {customer_role}'s written request, {vendor_role} will return or delete Personal Data at {customer_role}'s direction within {deletion_days} days and certify completion in writing. Retained copies required by law or archival controls remain subject to this DPA until deleted.

sections.heading: Audit and Assurance
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within {questionnaire_days} business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA. On at least {audit_days} days' notice, {customer_role} or its independent assessor may audit or inspect controls relevant to Personal Data once per calendar year and one additional time after a Personal Data Breach, regulatory request, or documented good-faith cause.

sections.heading: International Transfers
paragraphs: {vendor_role} will not transfer Personal Data to another country unless a lawful transfer mechanism is in place, including standard contractual clauses or another legally recognized safeguard where required. {vendor_role} will provide the transfer mechanism, destination country list, importer role, and transfer assessment inputs within {transfer_assessment_days} business days after {customer_role}'s written request.

sections.heading: Regulatory Cooperation
paragraphs: Within {regulatory_assist_days} business days after {customer_role}'s written request, {vendor_role} will provide data-flow summaries, processing records, security control mappings, transfer records, and subprocessor evidence for privacy impact assessments, regulator inquiries, consultations, and documentation needed to demonstrate compliance for the services.

sections.heading: Liability and Order of Precedence
paragraphs: Claims arising from {vendor_role}'s breach of this DPA are subject to the liability cap and exclusions stated in the master services agreement and are not subject to any lower cap, special exclusion, or separate damages waiver for data protection obligations. If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.

sections.heading: Term and Transition Assistance
paragraphs: This DPA begins with the first Processing of Personal Data and remains in effect for the duration of the services and for as long as {vendor_role} processes or retains Personal Data on behalf of {customer_role}. For up to {transition_days} days after termination, {vendor_role} will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.

distractors.span: {vendor_role} will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within {questionnaire_days} business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
distractors.why_benign: The audit canary says an annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance if follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: For up to {transition_days} days after termination, {vendor_role} will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
distractors.why_benign: The transition canary says a 30-day termination assistance period is acceptable.

distractors.span: If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.
distractors.why_benign: A DPA precedence clause for personal-data processing is customary and client-protective.

missing_info.context_anchor: The vendor will use subprocessors to process customer personal data for the production service.
